Milano, IT


***About us***


Chiesi is an international research\-focused biopharmaceutical group with 90 years’ experience, operating in more than 30 countries, with its Headquarter in Parma, Italy. More than 8,000 employees across the group are united by a singular purpose: promoting a healthier world for our people, patients, and the planet. This is what drives us as we research, develop, and market innovative drugs across our main therapeutic areas. Discover more here.


At Chiesi we are thinking generations ahead, driving sustainable innovation with purpose. This fosters a culture of reliability, transparency, and ethical behaviour at every level. As a Benefit Corporation and a certified B Corp, we have embedded sustainability in our bylaws and continuously measure our impact.


Diversity and inclusion are at the heart of who we are. We believe our differences make us stronger. We are a vibrant ecosystem of passionate, talented individuals united by strong values, each bringing unique perspectives that help us to continuously challenge the status quo for the better.

  

***Global Technical Operations \& Supply***


Global Technical Operations \& Supply (Global TechOp\&S) is Chiesi’s global manufacturing and supply area, responsible for ensuring consistent, outstanding quality, service and value across our end‑to‑end supply chain. Global TechOp\&S oversees the entire lifecycle of the drug: from process optimization and industrialization, to manufacturing, engineering, quality oversight, and supply chain management. Throughout this journey, Operational Excellence drives continuous improvement. This end‑to‑end model is powered by our four manufacturing sites \- Parma (IT), Blois (FR), Santana de Parnaíba (BR), and Nerviano (IT) \- together with our Virtual Plant network of external partners.


**This is what you will do**
----------------------------


As a Calibration Manager, you will lead the calibration program for both Utilities and Process Equipment across the manufacturing site, ensuring compliance with GMP requirements and company quality standards. Working closely with engineering, manufacturing, QA, Validation, and Maintenance teams, you will ensure that calibration activities are planned, executed, documented, and continuously improved.


**You will be responsible for**
-------------------------------


* Managing the site calibration program for Utilities and Process Equipment.
* Ensuring all calibration activities are performed in compliance with GMP, regulatory requirements, and internal procedures.
* Developing and maintaining the calibration master plan and ensuring timely execution of scheduled activities.
* Managing calibration records, certificates, and documentation to ensure data integrity and inspection readiness.
* Coordinating internal resources and external service providers performing calibration activities.
* Supporting qualification, validation, commissioning, and start\-up activities for new equipment and utilities.
* Defining calibration strategies, intervals, tolerances, and acceptance criteria based on risk assessment and regulatory expectations.
* Managing deviations, investigations, change controls, and CAPAs related to calibration systems.
* Ensuring the calibration status of critical instruments used in manufacturing, utilities, laboratories, and packaging areas.
* Monitoring KPIs related to calibration performance and implementing continuous improvement initiatives.
* Supporting internal and external audits as the subject matter expert for calibration processes.
* Leading and developing the calibration team while promoting a culture of safety, quality, and operational excellence.
**You will need to have**
-------------------------


* Degree in Engineering or a related scientific discipline or technical diploma
* Minimum 5 years of experience in calibration, maintenance, engineering, or technical services within the pharmaceutical or other GMP\-regulated industry.
* Strong knowledge of calibration principles for process instrumentation and utility systems.
* Experience with GMP regulations, Data Integrity principles, and pharmaceutical quality systems.
* Knowledge of utilities such as HVAC, Purified Water, Water for Injection (WFI), Clean Steam, Compressed Air
* Experience with process equipment including filling lines, packaging equipment, CIP/SIP systems, and manufacturing equipment.
* Familiarity with CMMS and calibration management systems.
* Experience managing contractors and external calibration service providers.
* Experience supporting regulatory inspections by FDA, AIFA
* Excellent knowledge of written and spoken English
**Location**
------------


Nerviano, Milan

----------------------


The minimum salary for this role is € 52\.000, aligned to our established salary framework. The final offer will reflect the candidate’s skills, experience, qualifications and internal equity considerations.


This role is eligible to participate in an annual bonus scheme, subject to company and individual performance.


In addition, we offer a comprehensive benefits package including pension, private medical insurance, wellbeing programme, flexible benefits programme.

No matter where your path starts at Chiesi, it leads to inspiring possibilities. Your future is our focus, and we are committed to nurturing your development in our dynamic, friendly environment with access to resources and training every step of the way.

**\#LI\-Hybrid**

In the execution of his day\-by\-day job the Inspector / Expediter shall carry out the project specific inspections and release the materials as detailed in the supplier’s Inspection and Test Plan (ITP) and as directed and instructed by the home office Inspection \& Expediting Coordinator. The Inspector / Expediter shall conduct: Witness, Hold, Monitor and Review activities as per the approved Supplier’s / Sub\-supplier’s ITP.


**Our Clients and Projects**

**Designing the future. Transforming the world.**

You will be working for major international clients in the energy market and material market, and you will be part of a team results focused that will provide technical solutions.


**What we can offer**

* **Meaningful and interesting projects** delivered to leaders of industry across chemical, petrochemical, oil\&gas and emerging energy sectors

* **Commitment to Diversity and Inclusion** ; we are an organisation actively committed to diversity and inclusion across our business

* **Global connections** ; join experts around the world who are at the leading edge of our industry, shaping the standards of our profession

  
**The key objectives for this role are:**

Duties specifically related to the function of Inspector are the following:

* Perform Vendor QC system audits and surveillances
* Be familiar with the material requisition requirements and Wood / client project specifications and drawings

* Review and analysis of supplier’s / sub\-supplier’s fabrication schedules
* Review and analysis of the progress status of all supplier’s / sub\-supplier’s activities, i.e. engineering, procurement of material, fabrication, inspection \& testing, packing and shipment.
* Issue of expediting reports after each visit, highlighting critical areas that require recovery or corrective actions
* Expediting of corrective actions required to resolve fabrication or delivery problems

Duties specifically related to the function of shop Expediter are the following:


* Review and analysis of supplier’s / sub\-supplier’s fabrication schedules
* Review and analysis of the progress status of all supplier’s / sub\-supplier’s activities, i.e. engineering, procurement of material, fabrication, inspection \& testing, packing and shipment.
* Issue of expediting reports after each visit, highlighting critical areas that require recovery or corrective actions
* Expediting of corrective actions required to resolve fabrication or delivery problems

***Global Technical Operations \& Supply***


Global Technical Operations \& Supply (Global TechOp\&S) is Chiesi’s global manufacturing and supply area, responsible for ensuring consistent, outstanding quality, service and value across our end‑to‑end supply chain. Global TechOp\&S oversees the entire lifecycle of the drug: from process optimization and industrialization, to manufacturing, engineering, quality oversight, and supply chain management. Throughout this journey, Operational Excellence drives continuous improvement. This end‑to‑end model is powered by our four manufacturing sites \- Parma (IT), Blois (FR), Santana de Parnaíba (BR), and Nerviano (IT) \- together with our Virtual Plant network of external partners.

----------------------------


You will coordinate maintenance activities for manufacturing and packaging equipment dedicated to pharmaceutical aerosol products (Metered Dose Inhalers – MDIs) and inhalers for metered\-dose powders. You will ensure the reliability, availability, and compliance of production assets while supporting uninterrupted manufacturing operations in accordance with GMP standards. * Coordinating preventive, predictive, and corrective maintenance activities for manufacturing and packaging equipment.

* Planning maintenance interventions in collaboration with production and planning to optimize manufacturing schedules.

* Monitoring equipment performance and identifying opportunities for reliability improvement through root cause analysis and continuous improvement initiatives.
* Supporting troubleshooting activities

* Ensuring maintenance activities comply with GMP requirements, safety regulations, and company engineering standards.
* Collaborating with Automation and Engineering teams to optimize equipment performance and implement technical improvements.
* Supporting regulatory inspections and internal audits by ensuring maintenance documentation is complete and inspection\-ready.
* Monitoring maintenance KPIs and implementing strategies to improve Overall Equipment Effectiveness (OEE), reliability, and maintenance efficiency.

-------------------------


* Technical diploma

* Strong knowledge of preventive, predictive, and corrective maintenance methodologies.
* Experience with pharmaceutical manufacturing and packaging equipment, preferably MDI or aerosol production lines.
* Good understanding of GMP regulations, engineering documentation, and pharmaceutical quality systems.
* Experience with CMMS systems
* Knowledge of pneumatic, mechanical, electrical, and automated production systems.
* Strong troubleshooting, analytical, and problem\-solving skills.
* English knowledge.
**We would prefer for you to have**
-----------------------------------


* Experience with PLC\-controlled equipment and industrial automation systems.
* Familiarity with Lean Manufacturing, TPM, Reliability Centered Maintenance (RCM), or Six Sigma methodologies.
* Experience supporting engineering projects and continuous improvement programs.
* Knowledge of risk management tools such as FMEA.
* Experience supporting regulatory inspections by FDA, AIFA.

Nerviano, Milan

Office**Job Description**


PPD is revolutionizing patient recruitment. As the clinical research division of Thermo Fisher Scientific, PPD has launched Patient First, a group dedicated to enhancing the patient experience from the first click to the final visit. Using human\-centered, digitally enabled solutions, Patient First optimizes patient recruitment and engagement, site operations, and quality data collection.

  

PPD is seeking a **Senior Patient Recruitment and Engagement Lead** (PREL) to join our Site Recruitment Optimization team. This team is made up of functional leads dedicated to recruitment on clinical studies, owning the entire process and overseeing implementation and effectiveness of the global recruitment strategy.


This is a home\-based position for candidates based in the United Kingdom, France, Bulgaria, Portugal, Serbia, Hungary, Ukraine or Italy with **global recruitment strategy and management experience** and a **comprehensive understanding of trial operations.** The successful candidate will also have **functional Lead experience within a team setting and customer management skills** (including presentation experience).

**Functional Lead:**

* Acts as a Functional Lead for recruitment and retention strategy and associated initiatives.
* Communicates with the project team including the Project Lead, Clinical Team Manager and CRA's.
* Ensure country and region\-appropriate methods are used and requirements met.
* Trains project team members on standard methodologies.
* Works to identify potential risks, develop contingency plans and escalates issues as appropriate.
* Acts as Subject Matter Authority (SMA) for patient recruitment and retention topics in all client communications.
* Tracks metrics and builds progress reports on enrollment targets and updates.

 **Strategy:**

* Develop and implement patient recruitment and retention strategies.
* Identification of potential barriers to recruitment and retention and develop risk mitigation plans.
* Work with the client and PPD's project team to identify strategic solutions for the study\-specific protocol/patient population.

 **Third Party\-Vendor Collaboration:**

* Coordinate the quality and performance of providers and address any issues.
* Ensure provider invoices are accurate and timely, oversight for vendor budget.
* Work with internal Finance to manage financial matters regarding providers.
* Coordinate communication between providers, clients, and the internal project team.

**Patient Diversity:**

* Collaborate with the Patient Diversity Team to ensure that the Diversity Plan is reflected within Study Recruitment and Retention Plan.
* Ensure any client diversity requirements are considered during strategy design and monitored throughout enrollment.
* Monitor patient diversity metrics throughout the study and report progress to clients.

 **Patient Enrollment Optimization:**

* Partner with Patient Enrollment Optimization Vendor leads to avoid redundancy of vendor oversight tasks and ensure compliance with the vendor's designated activities.
* Gather feedback from project teams and clients to review vendor performance and provide feedback to the Vendor Relationship Lead.
* Ensure that patient recruitment and retention successes and lessons learned are shared, including developing study\-specific examples for a cases studies library.
* Provide support for proposals and bid defenses around recruitment lead activities.

 **Education and Experience:**

* Bachelor's degree or equivalent and relevant formal academic / vocational qualification
* Previous direct patient recruitment experience that provides the knowledge, skills, and abilities to perform the job (comparable to 5\+ years)
* In some cases an equivalency, consisting of appropriate education, training, and/or directly related experience will be considered sufficient for an individual to meet the requirements of the role.

 **Knowledge, Skills and Abilities:**

* Excellent client\-facing and interpersonal skills, with the ability to establish and maintain positive relationships with clients, internal teams, and external vendors.
* Strong organizational and multitasking abilities, with the capacity to handle multiple projects simultaneously and meet deadlines.
* Analytical approach, with the ability to collect, analyze, and interpret data to drive informed decision\-making.
* Knowledge of healthcare regulations, patient privacy laws, and ethical considerations in patient recruitment.
* Proficiency in project management tools and software.


Location: United Kingdom, France, Bulgaria, Portugal, Serbia, Hungary, Ukraine or Italy. Relocation assistance is NOT provided.

* Must be legally authorized to work in your country without sponsorship.

  

At Thermo Fisher Scientific, we are committed to fostering a healthy and harmonious workplace for our employees. We understand the importance of creating an environment that allows individuals to excel. Please see below for the required qualifications for this position, which also includes the possibility of equivalent experience:

* Able to communicate, receive, and understand information and ideas with diverse groups of people in a comprehensible and reasonable manner.
* Able to work upright and stationary for typical working hours.
* Ability to use and learn standard office equipment and technology with proficiency.
* Able to perform successfully under pressure while prioritizing and handling multiple projects or activities.
* May require as\-needed travel (0\-20%).

Flex is the diversified manufacturing partner of choice that helps market\-leading brands design, build and deliver innovative products that improve the world.


A career at Flex offers the opportunity to make a difference and invest in your growth in a respectful, inclusive, and collaborative environment. If you are excited about a role but don't meet every bullet point, we encourage you to apply and join us to create the extraordinary.


**Job Summary**
---------------

We are looking for young professionals with a Master’s degree in Mechanical Engineering to join Flex Design Center in Milan.


In this role you will be part of an experienced team to pro\-actively support in the mechanical design of connected medical devices. You will execute technical assessments, perform simulations and implement innovative and cost\-effective solutions, from prototype development to full scale production.


**What a typical day looks like:**

* You will work in a multidisciplinary team to develop the mechanical components of medical devices.
* Perform feasibility studies and build proof of concept prototypes for assessment.
* Provide high quality technical reports supported by simulations and/or lab tests.

**Here is some of what you’ll need:**

* MS Degree in Mechanical Engineering or equivalent.
* Kinematics Mechanism and Production Technology Processes knowledge.
* CAD tool competence (with preference for CREO or SOLIDWORKS).

* Fluent in English and Italian.

**Here are a few of our preferred experiences:**

* Examples of personal projects/designs.

* 6 month Internship program (plus 6 month renewable.
* An International dynamic environment.
* Possibility to take part in the full project life cycle from initial design to mass production.

title: Medical Strategy Lead Rare
description:
**Make your mark for patients**

  

To strengthen our **Rare \& Epilepsy** department**,** we are looking for a talented profile to fill the position of: **Strategy Medical Lead Rare – Milan, Italy.**

 **About the role**  

You will need to eensure the highest possible level of scientific competencies in the local roles of the PVU and drive the appropriate scientific standards in the implementation of the strategy and tactical execution. You will guarantee a connection between Region, Area and local PVUs on long term strategic objectives, clinical development and cross boarder initiatives. You will be accountable for the creation of the medical part of the local PV (Brand) Plan mastering all medical content using the relevant inputs from EU.

 **You will work with**  

You will work closely with a broad network of stakeholders across local, European and global levels. Internally, you collaborate with the local medical team, including Medical Solution Leads and MSLs, whom you functionally lead and develop, as well as cross\-functional teams such as market access, regulatory and commercial to co\-create and implement Patient Value Plans. You are also in regular interaction with EU and global medical teams to ensure alignment on strategy, clinical development and cross\-border initiatives. Externally, you engage with healthcare professionals, investigators and research partners in the context of clinical studies and advisory activities, while also contributing to market access discussions and scientific interactions with key stakeholders.

 **What you will do**

* Highest scientific standards for strategy execution and tactical implementation
* Functional leadership of the local area Medical Community (Medical Solution Leads \& MSLs).
* Participation in the interdisciplinary development of the Patient Value Plans (PVP).
* Scientific input in the definition and shaping of local plans and input into MSL\-tactics
* Scientific adequacy and consistency of medical\-scientific solutions (projects and activities, including materials), that are initiated and implemented locally or as part of an EU strategy.
* Responsible for medical\-scientific knowledge of all local area team\-members and key to improving development of scientific skills, supported by other medical roles.
* Supports with his/her expertise the conception of medical solutions, like e.g. advisory boards, patient initiatives, added value\-concepts, etc..
* Responsible for preparing the area for future medical solutions in UCB´s pipeline (new assets \& therapy areas).
* Steering of local guideline\-initiatives and pipeline\-projects, implemented by MSLs.
* Key contact point for global and EU\-teams for clinical studies, Investigator Initiated Studies (ISS), Non\-Interventional Studies (NIS), Real World Evidence (RWE)\- projects and their implementation in the area.
* Supervising the production of publications, which are generated by local data.
* Responsible for high quality scientific content during development and provision of documents for marketing approval\- and pricing negotiations.
* Primary contact for the medical relevance of market access activities
* Connecting EU, area and region regarding long\-term strategic goals
* Scientific Reference for national market access initiatives

 **Interested? For this position you’ll need the following education, experience and skills**

* Bachelor's Degree in Life Sciences required, MD, PharmD, PhD preferred
* Fluent English communication (oral and written)
* 10\+ years in positions with stakeholder\-contact in the healthcare environment
* Knowledge of relevant regulatory and Access\-/ HTA\-regulations
* Proven coaching skills regarding individual and team development
* Proven strength in business planning, development and execution of tactical plans
* Knowledge in the areas of clinical studies, Investigator Initiated Studies (ISS), Non\- Interventional Studies (NIS), Real World Evidence (RWE)\-projects is beneficial
* Problem solving \& adaptability: managing complexity, ambiguity and sensitive situations
* Project management: ability to handle multiple priorities with strong execution discipline
* Personal effectiveness: autonomy, accountability, collaboration and continuous improvement mindset

  

Salary Range: 70\.400€ – 92\.400€

  

Are you ready to ‘go beyond’ to create value and make your mark for patients? If this sounds like you, then we would love to hear from you!

 **About us**  

UCB is a global biopharmaceutical company, focusing on neurology and immunology. We are over 9\.000 people in all four corners of the globe, inspired by patients and driven by science.

 **Why work with us?**  

At UCB, we don’t just complete tasks, we create value. We aren’t afraid to push forward, collaborate, and innovate to make our mark for patients. We have a caring, supportive culture where everyone feels included, respected, and has equal opportunities to do their best work. We ‘go beyond’ to create value for our patients, and always with a human focus, whether that’s on our patients, our employees, or our planet. Working for us, you will discover a place where you can grow, and have the freedom to carve your own career path to achieve your full potential.

  

At UCB, we’ve embraced a hybrid\-first approach to work, bringing teams together in local hubs to foster collaborative curiosity. Unless expressly stated in the description or precluded by the nature of the position, roles are hybrid with 40% of your time spent in the office.

  

UCB is an equal opportunity employer. All employment decisions will be made without regard to any characteristic protected by applicable laws.  

  

Should you require any adjustments to our process to assist you in demonstrating your strengths and capabilities contact us on EMEA\-Reasonable\_Accommodation@ucb.com. Please note should your enquiry not relate to adjustments; we will not be able to support you through this channel.

title: Manufacturing Process Scientist
description:
**Work Schedule**


Standard (Mon\-Fri)**Environmental Conditions**


Office**Job Description**

**How will you make an impact?**


Thermo Fisher Scientific Inc. is the world leader in serving science, with annual revenue of more than $40 billion. Our Mission is to enable our customers to make the world healthier, cleaner and safer. Whether our customers are accelerating life sciences research, solving complex analytical challenges, improving laboratory efficiency, advancing patient health through diagnostics, or developing and manufacturing life\-changing therapies, we are here to support them.


We are seeking a highly motivated **Manufacturing Process Scientist** to join the Pharmaceutical Development Services (PDS) team in Monza, Italy.


The Pharmaceutical Development Services (PDS) division specializes in clinical and small\-scale commercial contract manufacturing of sterile injectable drug products, including liquid and lyophilized formulations.


You will join the **Process Manufacturing Sciences** team, where scientists design, develop and optimize manufacturing processes for innovative therapies, working in close partnership with clients and cross\-functional teams. This role offers the opportunity to contribute to the successful delivery of life\-changing medicines while developing your scientific expertise in a dynamic and collaborative environment.

**What will you do?**


As a **Manufacturing Process Scientist**, you will support the development, optimization and technology transfer of sterile manufacturing processes into the cGMP manufacturing suites at the Monza site.


As the **Process Subject Matter Expert (SME)** for assigned projects, you will provide scientific leadership throughout the project lifecycle, partnering with clients and cross\-functional teams to develop robust, scalable and compliant manufacturing processes for clinical and commercial products.

**Your responsibilities will include:**

* Define, develop and optimize manufacturing processes for sterile injectable products based on product knowledge, development data and Quality Target Product Profile (QTPP).
* Prepare and review technical documentation, including manufacturing instructions, study protocols, technical reports, FMEA and GAP analyses.
* Drive the technical execution of assigned projects, supporting process development, scale\-up, technology transfer and manufacturing activities.
* Build strong scientific partnerships with clients, provide technical expertise and supporting process\-related decisions throughout the project lifecycle.
* Lead technical investigations, risk assessments and process optimization to ensure robust and compliant manufacturing processes.
* Collaborate with Manufacturing, MSAT, Quality, Engineering and other cross\-functional teams to ensure successful project execution.
* Present scientific data, project progress and technical recommendations to internal stakeholders and external clients.
* Support client meetings, audits and regulatory inspections as the process technical expert.
* Stay current with emerging technologies and scientific literature to drive innovation and continuous improvement.

**Qualifications**

**Minimum Qualifications**

* Bachelor's or Master's degree in Pharmacy, Pharmaceutical Chemistry and Technology, Biotechnology, Chemistry, Chemical Engineering, or another relevant scientific discipline.
* 2\-3 years of experience in sterile pharmaceutical process development and/or manufacturing is a must.
* 2\-3 years of experience with liquid, suspension and/or lyophilized drug products is also a must.
* Good knowledge of GMP requirements; experience with GLP is considered an advantage.
* 2\-3 years of experience preparing technical documentation such as manufacturing instructions, study protocols, technical reports, FMEA and GAP analyses is a must.
* Excellent written and verbal communication skills in English.

**Preferred Competencies**

* Strong scientific mindset with excellent analytical and problem\-solving skills.
* Ability to manage multiple projects and priorities in a fast\-paced environment.
* Excellent communication and presentation skills, with the ability to interact effectively with clients and cross\-functional teams.
* High attention to detail and commitment to quality.
* Curiosity, accountability and a continuous learning mindset.
* Ability to work both independently and collaboratively within multidisciplinary teams.

**Join us!**


At Thermo Fisher Scientific, each one of our more than 100,000 extraordinary minds has a unique story to tell. Join us and contribute to our singular mission—enabling our customers to make the world healthier, cleaner and safer.

title: Robotics Engineer
description:
**Join the Future of Regenerative Medicine**
--------------------------------------------


**Multiply Labs** is a robotics company on a mission to solve a significant bottleneck in modern medicine: **the manufacturing of life\-saving cell therapies.**



Currently, cell therapies are produced through manual, labor\-intensive processes. This keeps costs astronomical—often exceeding **$1M per patient**—and limits access to only a fraction of those in need. We are revolutionizing this process by building the world’s first modular autonomous robotic factories for pharmaceutical production.


### **Why This Matters**



We don’t just build robots; we build the infrastructure for the next generation of medicine. Our technology allows biopharma companies to scale production without redesigning their science, drastically lowering regulatory hurdles and bringing costs down to a level where these treatments can actually reach patients.


### **The Stack \& The Scale**


* **The Mission:** Build the world’s best robots to make life\-saving therapies accessible to all.
* **The Technology:** Advanced robotic orchestration, high\-precision automation, and cloud\-integrated hardware\-software systems.
* **The Location:** Headquartered in San Francisco, CA, with offices around the world. This role is based in Italy.
* **The Backing:** Supported by premier investors, including **Casdin Capital, Lux Capital, and Y Combinator.**

### **About The Role**



We are looking for a hands\-on Robotic Engineer who is passionate about applied technology and thrives in an operational environment. You will join our integration and testing team, working directly on the robotic cells that automate cell therapy manufacturing in a GMP setting. You will own the full cycle — from device configuration through full\-cell qualification — and play a key role in bringing our systems from design to deployment.


### **Key Responsibilities**


* Configure and test electromechanical devices in preparation for full robotic cell qualification
* Collaborate with electricians and assembly teams to diagnose and resolve issues during configuration and testing phases
* Execute test protocols using internal software, ensuring full traceability and quality of results
* Manage the organization and documentation of tested devices and materials
* Support the software integration of new devices and peripherals
* Actively participate in all stages of robotic cell qualification, from setup through final execution
### **Required Qualifications**


* **Programming:** Advanced proficiency in Python
* **Operating systems:** Linux and Windows; confident use of the terminal and development tools
* **Version control:** Git (daily use in a team environment)
* **Robotics:** hands\-on experience with industrial or collaborative robots, or a strong theoretical foundation in kinematics, dynamics, and control
* **Hands\-on mindset:** strong manual dexterity and ability to work physically with complex electromechanical systems
* **Problem\-solving:** empirical approach, comfort with experimentation, and ability to find solutions in the field
* **Technical reading:** ability to read electrical schematics, mechanical drawings, and product documentation
* **Organization:** autonomy, proactivity, and ability to manage priorities in a fast\-paced environment
* **Communication:** ability to clearly communicate technical ideas and schedule risks to distributed, international teams
* **Languages:** English B2\+ (written and spoken for technical documentation and US team communication); Italian C2 (Native/Bilingual proficiency).
* **Mobility:** valid driver's license (A/B), own vehicle required

#### ***Additional Preferred Experiences:***



* MSc in Mechanical Engineering, Mechatronics, Robotics, or related field
* PhD in Robotics or Autonomous Systems
* Experience in regulated environments (GMP, ISO 13485, or equivalent)
* Familiarity with ROS or control frameworks for collaborative robots
### **Perks \& Benefits**


### **Total Rewards \& Life at Multiply Labs**


* **Competitive Compensation:** Salary range of **€50,000\.00 \- €70,000** plus **meaningful early\-stage equity.** We want our team to have a real stake in the future we’re building.
* **Parental Support:** Robust **Paid Parental Leave** to support your family’s newest additions.
* **Referral bonuses** for helping us build a world\-class team.
* **Great people, meaningful work**, and a team that genuinely **believes in what we're building**.


Come build the world’s best robots—and help make life\-saving therapies accessible to all.



We actively seek candidates with diverse perspectives and lived experiences. We’re committed to building a team where everyone feels valued, respected, and empowered to do their best work.



Multiply Labs encourages candidates from all backgrounds to apply — even if you don’t meet every qualification. If you’re excited about our mission and think you can contribute, we’d love to hear from you.



We may use artificial intelligence (AI) tools to support parts of the hiring process, such as reviewing applications, analyzing resumes, or assessing responses and identifying potential inconsistencies or verification signals in application materials based on available information. These tools assist our recruitment team but do not replace human judgment. Final hiring decisions are ultimately made by humans. If you would like more information about how your data is processed, please contact us.
